curriculum.subject: Computer Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 6 chapters, and 20 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.
Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.
Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.
Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.
Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation.
Apply computational thinking by analyzing problems, developing algorithms using flowcharts/pseudocode, decomposing tasks, coding, testing, and debugging.
Demonstrate proficiency in the Python language including execution modes (interactive/script), character sets, tokens, variables, l-value/r-value concepts, and comments.
Differentiate between mutable and immutable data types including Numbers, Booleans, Strings, Lists, Tuples, Dictionaries, and None.
Utilize various operators (arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, identity, membership) with correct precedence rules for expression evaluation.
Implement control flow structures using conditional statements (if-else, if-elif-else) and iterative loops (for, while), including nested loops and break/continue statements.
Manipulate strings and sequences performing operations like concatenation, slicing, traversal, and utilizing built-in methods for case conversion, counting, searching, etc.

topics: Introduction to Computer System, hardware, software, input device, output device, CPU, memory (primary, cache and secondary), units of memory (bit, byte, KB, MB, GB, TB, PB)
System software (Operating systems, system utilities, device drivers), programming tools and language translators (assembler, compiler, and interpreter), application software
functions of the operating system, OS user interface
NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, NOT, truth tables and De Morgan's laws, Logic circuits
Binary, Octal, Decimal and Hexadecimal number system; conversion between number systems

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.

Functions: types of function (built-in functions, functions defined in module, user defined functions), creating user defined function, arguments and parameters, default parameters, positional parameters, function returning value(s), flow of execution, scope of a variable (global scope, local scope)
Exception Handling: Introduction, handling exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Introduction to files, types of files (Text file, Binary file, CSV file), relative and absolute paths
subtopics: Text file: opening a text file, text file open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), closing a text file, opening a file using with clause, writing/appending data to a text file using write() and writelines(), reading from a text file using read(), readline() and readlines(), seek and tell methods, manipulation of data in a text file

Binary file: basic operations on a binary file: open using file open modes (rb, rb+, wb, wb+, ab, ab+), close a binary file, import pickle module, dump() and load() method, read, write/create, search, append and update operations in a binary file
CSV file: import csv module, open / close csv file, write into a csv file using writer(),writerow(),writerows() and read from a csv file using reader()
Data Structure: Stack, operations on stack (push & pop), implementation of stack using list.

topics: Evolution of networking: introduction to computer networks, evolution of networking (ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET)

Data communication terminologies: concept of communication, components of data communication (sender,receiver, message, communication media, protocols), measuring capacity of communication media (bandwidth, data transfer rate), IP address, switching techniques (Circuit switching, Packet switching)
Transmission media: Wired communication media (Twisted pair cable, Co-axial cable, Fiber-optic cable), Wireless media (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared waves)
Network devices (Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card)
Network topologies and Network types: types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN), networking topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)
Network protocol: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP
Introduction to web services: WWW, Hyper Text Markup Language (HTML), Extensible Markup Language (XML), domain names, URL, website, web browser, web servers, web hosting
Database concepts: introduction to database concepts and its need
Relational data model: relation, attribute, tuple, domain, degree, cardinality, keys (candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key)

Structured Query Language: introduction, Data Definition Language and Data Manipulation Language, data type (char(n), varchar(n), int, float, date), constraints (not null, unique, primary key), create database, use database, show databases, drop database, show tables, create table, describe table, alter table (add and remove an attribute, add and remove primary key), drop table, insert, delete, select, operators (mathematical, relational and logical), aliasing, distinct clause, where clause, in, between, order by, meaning of null, is null, is not null, like, update command, delete command, aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count), group by, having clause, joins: cartesian product on two tables, equi-join and natural join

Interface of python with an SQL database: connecting SQL with Python, performing insert, update, delete queries using cursor, display data by using connect(), cursor(), execute(), commit(), fetchone(), fetchall(), rowcount, creating database connectivity applications, use of %s format specifier or format() to perform queries

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.

Functions: types of function (built-in functions, functions defined in module, user defined functions), creating user defined function, arguments and parameters, default parameters, positional parameters, function returning value(s), flow of execution, scope of a variable (global scope, local scope)
Exception Handling: Introduction, handling exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Introduction to files, types of files (Text file, Binary file, CSV file), relative and absolute paths
subtopics: Text file: opening a text file, text file open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), closing a text file, opening a file using with clause, writing/appending data to a text file using write() and writelines(), reading from a text file using read(), readline() and readlines(), seek and tell methods, manipulation of data in a text file

Binary file: basic operations on a binary file: open using file open modes (rb, rb+, wb, wb+, ab, ab+), close a binary file, import pickle module, dump() and load() method, read, write/create, search, append and update operations in a binary file
CSV file: import csv module, open / close csv file, write into a csv file using writer(),writerow(),writerows() and read from a csv file using reader()
Data Structure: Stack, operations on stack (push & pop), implementation of stack using list.

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.
Functions: types, arguments, parameters, scope, flow of execution
subtopics: Text file operations and open modes (r, w, a)
Binary file operations with pickle module
CSV file handling using csv module
Stack data structure implementation

topics: Evolution of networking: introduction to computer networks, evolution of networking (ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET)

Data communication terminologies: concept of communication, components of data communication (sender,receiver, message, communication media, protocols), measuring capacity of communication media (bandwidth, data transfer rate), IP address, switching techniques (Circuit switching, Packet switching)
Transmission media: Wired communication media (Twisted pair cable, Co-axial cable, Fiber-optic cable), Wireless media (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared waves)
Network devices (Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card)
Network topologies and Network types: types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN), networking topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)
Network protocol: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP
Introduction to web services: WWW, Hyper Text Markup Language (HTML), Extensible Markup Language (XML), domain names, URL, website, web browser, web servers, web hosting
Evolution of networking: ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET
Data communication terminologies and switching techniques
subtopics: Transmission media (Wired and Wireless)
Network devices and topologies
Types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN)

topics: Database concepts: introduction to database concepts and its need
Relational data model: relation, attribute, tuple, domain, degree, cardinality, keys (candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key)

Structured Query Language: introduction, Data Definition Language and Data Manipulation Language, data type (char(n), varchar(n), int, float, date), constraints (not null, unique, primary key), create database, use database, show databases, drop database, show tables, create table, describe table, alter table (add and remove an attribute, add and remove primary key), drop table, insert, delete, select, operators (mathematical, relational and logical), aliasing, distinct clause, where clause, in, between, order by, meaning of null, is null, is not null, like, update command, delete command, aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count), group by, having clause, joins: cartesian product on two tables, equi-join and natural join

Interface of python with an SQL database: connecting SQL with Python, performing insert, update, delete queries using cursor, display data by using connect(), cursor(), execute(), commit(), fetchone(), fetchall(), rowcount, creating database connectivity applications, use of %s format specifier or format() to perform queries

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Minimum of 20 Python programs in report file plus viva voce.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input welcome message, compare numbers, generate patterns using nested loops, series summation programs, check perfect/Armstrong/palindrome numbers, prime/composite check, Fibonacci series, GCD/LCM calculation, string character counting, list/tuple operations (swap elements, search), dictionary creation and filtering.

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XII)
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: SQL Queries Lab (Class XII)
practicals.details: 4 queries based on one or two tables.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XII)
practicals.details: Minimum of 15 Python programs, minimum 5 sets of SQL queries using one or two tables, and minimum 4 programs based on Python-SQL connectivity.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - File Handling
practicals.details: Read text file line by line, count vowels/consonants in files, remove lines containing 'a', create binary file for search/update operations, random number generator (dice simulation), implement stack using list.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - CSV
practicals.details: Create a CSV file with user-id and password, read and search for passwords.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Database Management
practicals.details: Create student table, implement ALTER, UPDATE, ORDER BY, DELETE commands. Perform GROUP BY operations to find min, max, sum, count, and average.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Python-SQL Integration
practicals.details: Integrate SQL with Python by importing suitable module to perform database operations.

projects.title: Class XI Project
projects.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
projects.details: Project that uses most of the concepts learnt in Class XI.

projects.title: Class XII Project
projects.details: Create something tangible and useful using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Examples include GST invoice generators, school software, disabled student aids, mobile applications, or games.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 10, Practical 10), U2 (Theory 45, Practical 60), U3 (Theory 15, Practical -)
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 40, Practical 50), U2 (Theory 10, Practical -), U3 (Theory 20, Practical 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI and XII Theory papers based on unit weightage distribution provided in the syllabus.
Practical exams include Lab Tests, Report Files with Viva Voce.
competency_weightage: Competencies assessed through problem-solving steps (analyzing, algorithm development), coding, testing, debugging, and application of concepts like file handling, SQL connectivity, and network protocols.
Weightage reflects unit marks distribution: Computational Thinking/Programming carries the highest weight in both classes.
bloom_levels: Assessment covers understanding (explain cyber ethics), applying (write Python programs for specific tasks), analyzing (debugging errors), creating (project work, generating patterns).
Specific Bloom levels inferred from practical requirements: Logic application (60%), Documentation (20%), Code Quality (20%).
internal_assessment: Class XI Lab Test (12 marks): Python program evaluation.
Class XII Lab Test (8 marks for Class XII, 4 marks SQL queries).
Viva Voce included in practical assessment.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Report File with minimum 20 Python programs and Viva Voce.
Class XII: Report File with minimum 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries (one/two tables), and 4 programs on Python-SQL connectivity.
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible/useful application using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Group size (2-3 students). Marks allocation included in practical total.

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Minimum of 20 Python programs in report file plus viva voce.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input welcome message, compare numbers, generate patterns using nested loops, series summation programs, check perfect/Armstrong/palindrome numbers, prime/composite check, Fibonacci series, GCD/LCM calculation, string character counting, list/tuple operations (swap elements, search), dictionary creation and filtering.

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XII)
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: SQL Queries Lab (Class XII)
practicals.details: 4 queries based on one or two tables.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XII)
practicals.details: Minimum of 15 Python programs, minimum 5 sets of SQL queries using one or two tables, and minimum 4 programs based on Python-SQL connectivity.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - File Handling
practicals.details: Read text file line by line, count vowels/consonants in files, remove lines containing 'a', create binary file for search/update operations, random number generator (dice simulation), implement stack using list.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - CSV
practicals.details: Create a CSV file with user-id and password, read and search for passwords.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Database Management
practicals.details: Create student table, implement ALTER, UPDATE, ORDER BY, DELETE commands. Perform GROUP BY operations to find min, max, sum, count, and average.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Python-SQL Integration
practicals.details: Integrate SQL with Python by importing suitable module to perform database operations.

projects.title: Class XI Project
projects.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
projects.details: Project that uses most of the concepts learnt in Class XI.

projects.title: Class XII Project
projects.details: Create something tangible and useful using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Examples include GST invoice generators, school software, disabled student aids, mobile applications, or games.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 10, Practical 10), U2 (Theory 45, Practical 60), U3 (Theory 15, Practical -)
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 40, Practical 50), U2 (Theory 10, Practical -), U3 (Theory 20, Practical 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI and XII Theory papers based on unit weightage distribution provided in the syllabus.
Practical exams include Lab Tests, Report Files with Viva Voce.
competency_weightage: Competencies assessed through problem-solving steps (analyzing, algorithm development), coding, testing, debugging, and application of concepts like file handling, SQL connectivity, and network protocols.
Weightage reflects unit marks distribution: Computational Thinking/Programming carries the highest weight in both classes.
bloom_levels: Assessment covers understanding (explain cyber ethics), applying (write Python programs for specific tasks), analyzing (debugging errors), creating (project work, generating patterns).
Specific Bloom levels inferred from practical requirements: Logic application (60%), Documentation (20%), Code Quality (20%).
internal_assessment: Class XI Lab Test (12 marks): Python program evaluation.
Class XII Lab Test (8 marks for Class XII, 4 marks SQL queries).
Viva Voce included in practical assessment.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Report File with minimum 20 Python programs and Viva Voce.
Class XII: Report File with minimum 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries (one/two tables), and 4 programs on Python-SQL connectivity.
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible/useful application using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Group size (2-3 students). Marks allocation included in practical total.

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.

Functions: types of function (built-in functions, functions defined in module, user defined functions), creating user defined function, arguments and parameters, default parameters, positional parameters, function returning value(s), flow of execution, scope of a variable (global scope, local scope)
Exception Handling: Introduction, handling exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Introduction to files, types of files (Text file, Binary file, CSV file), relative and absolute paths
subtopics: Text file: opening a text file, text file open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), closing a text file, opening a file using with clause, writing/appending data to a text file using write() and writelines(), reading from a text file using read(), readline() and readlines(), seek and tell methods, manipulation of data in a text file

Binary file: basic operations on a binary file: open using file open modes (rb, rb+, wb, wb+, ab, ab+), close a binary file, import pickle module, dump() and load() method, read, write/create, search, append and update operations in a binary file
CSV file: import csv module, open / close csv file, write into a csv file using writer(),writerow(),writerows() and read from a csv file using reader()
Data Structure: Stack, operations on stack (push & pop), implementation of stack using list.

topics: Evolution of networking: introduction to computer networks, evolution of networking (ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET)

Data communication terminologies: concept of communication, components of data communication (sender,receiver, message, communication media, protocols), measuring capacity of communication media (bandwidth, data transfer rate), IP address, switching techniques (Circuit switching, Packet switching)
Transmission media: Wired communication media (Twisted pair cable, Co-axial cable, Fiber-optic cable), Wireless media (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared waves)
Network devices (Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card)
Network topologies and Network types: types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN), networking topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)
Network protocol: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP
Introduction to web services: WWW, Hyper Text Markup Language (HTML), Extensible Markup Language (XML), domain names, URL, website, web browser, web servers, web hosting
Database concepts: introduction to database concepts and its need
Relational data model: relation, attribute, tuple, domain, degree, cardinality, keys (candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key)

Structured Query Language: introduction, Data Definition Language and Data Manipulation Language, data type (char(n), varchar(n), int, float, date), constraints (not null, unique, primary key), create database, use database, show databases, drop database, show tables, create table, describe table, alter table (add and remove an attribute, add and remove primary key), drop table, insert, delete, select, operators (mathematical, relational and logical), aliasing, distinct clause, where clause, in, between, order by, meaning of null, is null, is not null, like, update command, delete command, aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count), group by, having clause, joins: cartesian product on two tables, equi-join and natural join

Interface of python with an SQL database: connecting SQL with Python, performing insert, update, delete queries using cursor, display data by using connect(), cursor(), execute(), commit(), fetchone(), fetchall(), rowcount, creating database connectivity applications, use of %s format specifier or format() to perform queries

topics: Revision of Python topics covered in Class XI.

Functions: types of function (built-in functions, functions defined in module, user defined functions), creating user defined function, arguments and parameters, default parameters, positional parameters, function returning value(s), flow of execution, scope of a variable (global scope, local scope)
Exception Handling: Introduction, handling exceptions using try-except-finally blocks
Introduction to files, types of files (Text file, Binary file, CSV file), relative and absolute paths
subtopics: Text file: opening a text file, text file open modes (r, r+, w, w+, a, a+), closing a text file, opening a file using with clause, writing/appending data to a text file using write() and writelines(), reading from a text file using read(), readline() and readlines(), seek and tell methods, manipulation of data in a text file

Binary file: basic operations on a binary file: open using file open modes (rb, rb+, wb, wb+, ab, ab+), close a binary file, import pickle module, dump() and load() method, read, write/create, search, append and update operations in a binary file
CSV file: import csv module, open / close csv file, write into a csv file using writer(),writerow(),writerows() and read from a csv file using reader()
Data Structure: Stack, operations on stack (push & pop), implementation of stack using list.

topics: Evolution of networking: introduction to computer networks, evolution of networking (ARPANET, NSFNET, INTERNET)

Data communication terminologies: concept of communication, components of data communication (sender,receiver, message, communication media, protocols), measuring capacity of communication media (bandwidth, data transfer rate), IP address, switching techniques (Circuit switching, Packet switching)
Transmission media: Wired communication media (Twisted pair cable, Co-axial cable, Fiber-optic cable), Wireless media (Radio waves, Micro waves, Infrared waves)
Network devices (Modem, Ethernet card, RJ45, Repeater, Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway, WIFI card)
Network topologies and Network types: types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN), networking topologies (Bus, Star, Tree)
Network protocol: HTTP, FTP, PPP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP
Introduction to web services: WWW, Hyper Text Markup Language (HTML), Extensible Markup Language (XML), domain names, URL, website, web browser, web servers, web hosting

units.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
topics: Familiarization with the basics of Python programming
Expressions, statement, type conversion, and input/output
subtopics: Steps for Problem-solving (Analyzing the problem, developing an algorithm, coding, testing, and debugging), representation of algorithms using flowchart and pseudocode, decomposition

Introduction to Python, Features of Python, executing a simple 'hello world' program, execution modes: interactive mode and script mode, Python character set, Python tokens( keyword, identifier, literal, operator, punctuator), variables, concept of l-value and r-value, use of comments
Number(integer, floating point,complex), boolean, sequence(string, list, tuple), None, Mapping(dictionary), mutable and immutable data types.
arithmetic operators, relational operators, logical operators, assignment operators, augmented assignment operators, identity operators (is, is not), membership operators (in not in)
precedence of operators, expression, evaluation of an expression, type-conversion (explicit and implicit conversion), accepting data as input from the console and displaying output.
syntax errors, logical errors, and run-time errors
introduction, use of indentation, sequential flow, conditional and iterative flow
if, if-else, if-elif-else, flowcharts, simple programs: e.g.: absolute value, sort 3 numbers and divisibility of a number.
for loop, range(), while loop, flowcharts, break and continue statements, nested loops, suggested programs: generating pattern, summation of series, finding the factorial of a positive number, etc.

introduction, string operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a string using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), capitalize(), title(), lower(), upper(), count(), find(), index(), endswith(), startswith(), isalnum(), isalpha(), isdigit(), islower(), isupper(), isspace(),lstrip(), rstrip(), strip(), replace(), join(), partition(), split()

introduction, indexing, list operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing), traversing a list using loops, built-in functions/methods–len(), list(), append(), extend(), insert(), count(), index(), remove(), pop(), reverse(), sort(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); nested lists, suggested programs: finding the maximum, minimum, mean of numeric values stored in a list; linear search on list of numbers and counting the frequency of elements in a list.

introduction, indexing, tuple operations (concatenation, repetition, membership and slicing); built-in functions/methods – len(), tuple(), count(), index(), sorted(), min(), max(), sum(); tuple assignment, nested tuple; suggested programs: finding the minimum, maximum, mean of values stored in a tuple; linear search on a tuple of numbers, counting the frequency of elements in a tuple.

introduction, accessing items in a dictionary using keys, mutability of a dictionary (adding a new term, modifying an existing item), traversing a dictionary, built-in functions/methods – len(), dict(), keys(), values(), items(), get(), update(), del, clear(), fromkeys(), copy(), pop(), popitem(), setdefault(), max(), min(), sorted(); Suggested programs: count the number of times a character appears in a given string using a dictionary, create a dictionary with names of employees, their salary and access them.

Importing module using 'import <module>' and using from statement, importing math module (pi, e, sqrt(), ceil(), floor(), pow(), fabs(), sin(), cos(), tan()); random module (random(), randint(), randrange()), statistics module (mean(), median(), mode()).

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Minimum of 20 Python programs in report file plus viva voce.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input welcome message, compare numbers, generate patterns using nested loops, series summation programs, check perfect/Armstrong/palindrome numbers, prime/composite check, Fibonacci series, GCD/LCM calculation, string character counting, list/tuple operations (swap elements, search), dictionary creation and filtering.

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XII)
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: SQL Queries Lab (Class XII)
practicals.details: 4 queries based on one or two tables.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XII)
practicals.details: Minimum of 15 Python programs, minimum 5 sets of SQL queries using one or two tables, and minimum 4 programs based on Python-SQL connectivity.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - File Handling
practicals.details: Read text file line by line, count vowels/consonants in files, remove lines containing 'a', create binary file for search/update operations, random number generator (dice simulation), implement stack using list.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - CSV
practicals.details: Create a CSV file with user-id and password, read and search for passwords.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Database Management
practicals.details: Create student table, implement ALTER, UPDATE, ORDER BY, DELETE commands. Perform GROUP BY operations to find min, max, sum, count, and average.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Python-SQL Integration
practicals.details: Integrate SQL with Python by importing suitable module to perform database operations.

projects.title: Class XI Project
projects.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
projects.details: Project that uses most of the concepts learnt in Class XI.

projects.title: Class XII Project
projects.details: Create something tangible and useful using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Examples include GST invoice generators, school software, disabled student aids, mobile applications, or games.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 10, Practical 10), U2 (Theory 45, Practical 60), U3 (Theory 15, Practical -)
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 40, Practical 50), U2 (Theory 10, Practical -), U3 (Theory 20, Practical 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI and XII Theory papers based on unit weightage distribution provided in the syllabus.
Practical exams include Lab Tests, Report Files with Viva Voce.
competency_weightage: Competencies assessed through problem-solving steps (analyzing, algorithm development), coding, testing, debugging, and application of concepts like file handling, SQL connectivity, and network protocols.
Weightage reflects unit marks distribution: Computational Thinking/Programming carries the highest weight in both classes.
bloom_levels: Assessment covers understanding (explain cyber ethics), applying (write Python programs for specific tasks), analyzing (debugging errors), creating (project work, generating patterns).
Specific Bloom levels inferred from practical requirements: Logic application (60%), Documentation (20%), Code Quality (20%).
internal_assessment: Class XI Lab Test (12 marks): Python program evaluation.
Class XII Lab Test (8 marks for Class XII, 4 marks SQL queries).
Viva Voce included in practical assessment.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Report File with minimum 20 Python programs and Viva Voce.
Class XII: Report File with minimum 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries (one/two tables), and 4 programs on Python-SQL connectivity.
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible/useful application using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Group size (2-3 students). Marks allocation included in practical total.

competency_groups.title: Computer Systems and Organisation Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding computer architecture, software types, operating systems, logic circuits, number systems, and encoding schemes.
competencies: Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.
Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.
Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.
Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.
Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation.

competency_groups.title: Computational Thinking and Programming Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to problem-solving methodologies, Python programming fundamentals, data types, operators, control structures, and standard library usage.
competencies: Apply computational thinking by analyzing problems, developing algorithms using flowcharts/pseudocode, decomposing tasks, coding, testing, and debugging.
Demonstrate proficiency in the Python language including execution modes (interactive/script), character sets, tokens, variables, l-value/r-value concepts, and comments.
Differentiate between mutable and immutable data types including Numbers, Booleans, Strings, Lists, Tuples, Dictionaries, and None.
Utilize various operators (arithmetic, relational, logical, assignment, identity, membership) with correct precedence rules for expression evaluation.
Implement control flow structures using conditional statements (if-else, if-elif-else) and iterative loops (for, while), including nested loops and break/continue statements.
Manipulate strings and sequences performing operations like concatenation, slicing, traversal, and utilizing built-in methods for case conversion, counting, searching, etc.
Manage data structures by implementing list, tuple, and dictionary operations such as indexing, appending, extending, sorting, filtering, and nested structure handling.
Identify and handle different types of errors: syntax, logical, and run-time errors.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I

competency_groups.title: Society, Law, and Ethics Competencies (Class XI)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to digital citizenship, legal frameworks, cyber safety, ethical considerations in technology use.
competencies: Adhere to net etiquettes and communication protocols on social media platforms.
Understand Intellectual Property Rights including copyright, patents, trademarks, open source licensing (GPL, Apache), and identify violations like plagiarism.
Recognize various cyber crimes such as hacking, phishing, ransomware, eavesdropping, cyber bullying, and fraud.
Implement cyber safety practices including identity protection, confidentiality measures, and safe web browsing habits.
Identify types of malware (viruses, trojans, adware) and their impacts on systems.
Understand the importance of E-waste management regarding proper disposal of electronic gadgets.
Analyze gender and disability issues in the context of teaching and using computers.

competency_groups.title: Advanced Programming Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to advanced Python concepts including functions, exception handling, file operations, and data structures.
competencies: Apply the concept of functions by defining user-defined functions with arguments, parameters (positional/default), return values, scope management (global/local), and flow execution.

competency_groups.title: File Handling Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to reading, writing, and manipulating text, binary, and CSV files.
competencies: Perform file operations on Text Files including opening modes (r, w, a), using context managers ('with' clause), read/write methods, seek/tell positioning, and data manipulation.

competency_groups.title: Binary and CSV File Handling Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to handling binary files using pickle module and managing CSV data.
competencies: Execute basic operations on Binary Files including open modes, close methods, importing the pickle module for dump/load operations, search, append, update, and read/write functionalities.

competency_groups.title: Data Structures Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to implementing stack data structures.
competencies: Implement the Stack data structure using lists, performing push and pop operations.

competency_groups.title: Exception Handling Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to error management in Python programs.
competencies: Handle exceptions using try-except-finally blocks to manage runtime errors gracefully.

competency_groups.title: Computer Networks Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to networking evolution, communication models, media, devices, topologies, protocols, and web services.
competencies: Trace the evolution of computer networks from ARPANET/NSFNET to the INTERNET.

competency_groups.title: Data Communication Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to data communication components and metrics.
competencies: Explain the concept of data communication including sender, receiver, message, media, protocols, bandwidth, transfer rates, IP addressing, and switching techniques.

competency_groups.title: Transmission Media and Devices Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to physical media types and network hardware.
competencies: Differentiate between wired transmission media (Twisted pair, Co-axial, Fiber-optic) and wireless media (Radio waves, Microwaves, Infrared).

competency_groups.title: Network Topologies and Types Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to network classifications and physical/logical layouts.
competencies: Identify types of networks (PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN) and topologies (Bus, Star, Tree).

competency_groups.title: Network Protocols and Web Services Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to communication protocols and web infrastructure.
competencies: Explain network protocols including HTTP, FTP, SMTP, TCP/IP, POP3, HTTPS, TELNET, VoIP.

competency_groups.title: Introduction to Web Services Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to web technologies and infrastructure.
competencies: Understand the components of web services including WWW, HTML, XML, domain names, URLs, websites, browsers, servers, and hosting.

competency_groups.title: Database Concepts Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to fundamental database theory and relational models.
competencies: Explain the need for databases and core concepts including relations, attributes, tuples, domains.

competency_groups.title: Relational Data Model Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to keys and relational properties.
competencies: Define degree, cardinality, and various types of keys: candidate key, primary key, alternate key, foreign key.

competency_groups.title: SQL Data Definition and Manipulation Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to creating, altering, dropping database objects and managing data.
competencies: Execute SQL commands for Database Management: create/use/drop databases, show tables.

competency_groups.title: SQL Table Operations Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to table structure modification and data manipulation.
competencies: Perform Data Definition Language operations: create/describe/alter tables, add/remove attributes or primary keys.

competency_groups.title: SQL Querying and Filtering Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to retrieving data using SELECT statements.
competencies: Execute Data Manipulation Language queries: insert, delete, select with operators, aliasing, distinct clause.

competency_groups.title: Advanced SQL Query Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to complex querying including filtering, sorting, grouping, and joining.
competencies: Construct advanced queries using where clause, in/between operators, order by, is null/is not null, like operator.

competency_groups.title: SQL Aggregation and Joins Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to aggregate functions and table joins.
competencies: Utilize aggregate functions (max, min, avg, sum, count) with group by and having clauses.

competency_groups.title: Python-SQL Interface Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to integrating Python with SQL databases.
competencies: Establish database connectivity using connect(), cursor() methods.

competency_groups.title: Python-SQL Operations Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to executing queries and handling results in Python.
competencies: Perform insert, update, delete operations using cursor.execute() with format specifiers (%s or format()).

competency_groups.title: Python-SQL Data Retrieval Competencies (Class XII)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to fetching and displaying data from SQL databases via Python.
competencies: Display retrieved data using fetchone(), fetchall() methods.

marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 10, Practical 10), U2 (Theory 45, Practical 60), U3 (Theory 15, Practical -)
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise breakdown: U1 (Theory 40, Practical 50), U2 (Theory 10, Practical -), U3 (Theory 20, Practical 20)
question_paper_design: Class XI and XII Theory papers based on unit weightage distribution provided in the syllabus.
Practical exams include Lab Tests, Report Files with Viva Voce.
competency_weightage: Competencies assessed through problem-solving steps (analyzing, algorithm development), coding, testing, debugging, and application of concepts like file handling, SQL connectivity, and network protocols.
Weightage reflects unit marks distribution: Computational Thinking/Programming carries the highest weight in both classes.
bloom_levels: Assessment covers understanding (explain cyber ethics), applying (write Python programs for specific tasks), analyzing (debugging errors), creating (project work, generating patterns).
Specific Bloom levels inferred from practical requirements: Logic application (60%), Documentation (20%), Code Quality (20%).
internal_assessment: Class XI Lab Test (12 marks): Python program evaluation.
Class XII Lab Test (8 marks for Class XII, 4 marks SQL queries).
Viva Voce included in practical assessment.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Report File with minimum 20 Python programs and Viva Voce.
Class XII: Report File with minimum 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries (one/two tables), and 4 programs on Python-SQL connectivity.
project_assessment: Class XI Project: Uses concepts learnt; marks allocated in practical section.
Class XII Project: Tangible/useful application using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Group size (2-3 students). Marks allocation included in practical total.

outcomes: Develop a basic understanding of computer system architecture including hardware components (CPU, memory units), input/output devices, and storage hierarchy.
Explain the distinction between system software, application software, and programming tools including language translators.
Describe the functions of an operating system and its user interface capabilities.
Apply Boolean logic principles including truth tables, De Morgan's laws, and basic logic circuit design (NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR).
Perform conversions between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal number systems.
Identify and explain encoding schemes such as ASCII, ISCII, Unicode (UTF8, UTF32) for character representation.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
outcomes: Manage data structures by implementing list, tuple, and dictionary operations such as indexing, appending, extending, sorting, filtering, and nested structure handling.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I

Adhere to net etiquettes and communication protocols on social media platforms.

Identify types of malware (viruses, trojans, adware) and their impacts on systems.

projects.details: Create something tangible and useful using Python file handling or Python-SQL connectivity. Examples include GST invoice generators, school software, disabled student aids, mobile applications, or games.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XI)
practicals.unit_name: Unit 2: Computational Thinking and Programming - I
practicals.details: Input welcome message, compare numbers, generate patterns using nested loops, series summation programs, check perfect/Armstrong/palindrome numbers, prime/composite check, Fibonacci series, GCD/LCM calculation, string character counting, list/tuple operations (swap elements, search), dictionary creation and filtering.

practicals.title: Lab Test (Class XII)
practicals.details: Python program evaluation based on logic, documentation, and code quality.

practicals.title: SQL Queries Lab (Class XII)
practicals.details: 4 queries based on one or two tables.

practicals.title: Report File + Viva (Class XII)
practicals.details: Minimum of 15 Python programs, minimum 5 sets of SQL queries using one or two tables, and minimum 4 programs based on Python-SQL connectivity.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - File Handling
practicals.details: Read text file line by line, count vowels/consonants in files, remove lines containing 'a', create binary file for search/update operations, random number generator (dice simulation), implement stack using list.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - CSV
practicals.details: Create a CSV file with user-id and password, read and search for passwords.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Database Management
practicals.details: Create student table, implement ALTER, UPDATE, ORDER BY, DELETE commands. Perform GROUP BY operations to find min, max, sum, count, and average.

practicals.title: Suggested Practical List (Class XII) - Python-SQL Integration
practicals.details: Integrate SQL with Python by importing suitable module to perform database operations.
